Joash is more zealous about repairing the temple than Jehoiada himself. It is easier to build temples than to be like temples to God. But repairing places for public worship is a good work that all should promote. Many good works would be done that now remain undone if active people would put them forward.

See what a great judgment on any prince or people the death of godly, zealous, useful men is. See how necessary it is that we act in religion from inward principle. Then the loss of a parent, a minister, or a friend will not mean losing our religion. Often both princes and common people have been flattered to their ruin. True grace alone will enable a man to bring forth fruit until the end. Zechariah, the son of Jehoiada, being filled with the Spirit of prophecy, stood up and told the people of their sin. This is the work of ministers, by the word of God, as a lamp and a light, to discover men’s sins and explain God's actions. They stoned Zechariah to death in the court of the house of the Lord. Observe the dying martyr’s words: The Lord look upon it and require it! This came not from a spirit of revenge but a spirit of prophecy. God struck Joash with great diseases, of body, or mind, or both, before the Syrians departed from him. If vengeance pursues men, the end of one trouble will be only the beginning of another. His own servants killed him. These judgments are called the burdens laid upon him, for the wrath of God is a heavy burden, too heavy for any man to bear. May God help us to take warning, to be upright in heart, and to persevere in his ways until the end.
